title: NC / share-alike / license-compatibility acceptance policy
context: The license gate (gate-003) defers NonCommercial (NC) and share-alike (SA) edge cases to this explicit policy. CC-BY-SA sources propagate the share-alike obligation to derived banks (CC-BY-SA-4.0 output); NC sources are ambiguous for downstream commercial reuse (a teacher at a fee-charging school) and must be explicitly resolved rather than guessed at. Mixing SA and non-SA sources in one bank requires a compatibility ruling. This policy must be merged before any bank is authored from an NC or SA source, and must be referenced by the license gate checklist.
objective: Establish a clear, committed acceptance policy for NonCommercial and share-alike OER sources, specifying when they may be used, what output license obligations they trigger, and how mixing is handled.
acceptanceCriteria: States the default stance on NC sources: default is exclude/escalate for commercial-reuse ambiguity, with criteria for any exceptions and who may grant them.
States that CC-BY-SA sources are accepted with share-alike propagation: any bank derived from a CC-BY-SA source is licensed CC-BY-SA-4.0, recorded per bank.
Addresses mixing: specifies whether SA and non-SA sources may be mixed in one bank and, if so, the compatibility rules and resulting output license.
Policy is versioned, references the gate-003 checklist, and must be merged before any authoring from an NC or SA source.
Document is CC-BY-4.0 licensed; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: quiz-bank-open-gate-003 (license gate checklist — must be consistent with this policy)
Creative Commons compatibility chart and license texts
output: A committed NC/share-alike/license-compatibility acceptance policy merged before any authoring from NC or SA sources.
